description: First Ecumenical Council, addressed Arianism, affirmed Trinity
Second Ecumenical Council, affirmed deity of Holy Spirit
Third Ecumenical Council, affirmed Mary as Theotokos
Fourth Ecumenical Council, defined two natures of Christ
Split between Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches
Luther posted theses, began Protestant Reformation
Catholic Counter-Reformation council, ran until 1563
KJV commissioned by King James I of England
Reformed Presbyterian confession of faith
Defined papal infallibility
Vatican II, ran until 1965, major Catholic reform council
Early form around 390 AD, current form 8th century
Formulated at Council of Nicaea 325 AD
Born 354 AD, died 430 AD, bishop and theologian
Born 1225 AD, died 1274 AD, Summa Theologica author
Born 1509 AD, died 1564 AD, Reformed theology founder
Born 1703 AD, died 1791 AD, Methodist movement founder
Council of Carthage 397 AD recognized 27-book NT canon
First major book printed with movable type
Traditionally dated ~33 AD, birth of Christian church
